description: Evals for the callable-only /claudia rules utility skill. Validates dry-run preview + gate + idempotent injection, detect-awareness, and graceful handling of missing rule subsets.

tests.name: python-create-claude-md
tests.scenario_setup: Fresh Python repo at /tmp/rules-test-python-create with pyproject.toml and src/. No CLAUDE.md exists.
expected_behavior: Run `claudia rules inject --dry-run` and parse JSON envelope.
On Apply, run injection and emit one-line summary.
assertions: preview rendered with action=create and project_type=python
gate prompt shown before any disk write
CLAUDE.md created at repo root after apply

tests.name: idempotent-refresh
tests.scenario_setup: Python repo at /tmp/rules-test-idempotent-refresh where `claudia rules inject` was already applied. CLAUDE.md has the sentinel-bounded block already.
expected_behavior: Skill does NOT prompt for backup (sentinel block already exists = previously injected).
Running the skill twice produces an identical CLAUDE.md (zero diff).
assertions: no backup prompt is suggested for a previously-injected file
running the skill again yields zero functional diff (diff exit code 0)
gate is still shown even on no-op refresh

tests.name: mixed-repo-detect
tests.scenario_setup: Mixed repo at /tmp/rules-test-mixed-detect with both pyproject.toml AND main.nf. `claudia detect` returns mixed. No `rules/nextflow/` exists in plugin today.
expected_behavior: rule_count reflects common + python only (nextflow subset silently skipped).
Skill clearly communicates to the user that nextflow was skipped (no rules/nextflow/ in plugin yet).
assertions: preview contains python rules and zero nextflow rules
user message mentions that nextflow rules were skipped (or that only common+python were included)

scoring.method: binary per assertion, average per test
